features.title: Customer ledgers

features.title: Supplier ledgers

features.title: Statement of account

features.title: Always reconciled
features.desc: Ledgers update from the same entries as your invoices and payments, so they always agree.

sections.title: What is a ledger?
sections.body: A ledger is the running record of all transactions affecting an account, with a balance carried forward after each entry. A customer ledger shows every invoice you raised, every payment received and every credit note, ending in the balance they owe. A statement of account is that ledger presented to the customer for a period. Ledgers are the backbone of knowing who owes what, and to whom you owe.

sections.title: One source of truth
sections.body: In Paper & Pen, ledgers are not a separate spreadsheet, they are built from the same invoices, payments and credit notes you already create.
items: Open any customer or supplier to see their full ledger
Every invoice, payment, credit and debit note appears with a running balance
Filter by date range for a clean statement of account
Send the statement by WhatsApp or email in one tap
Balances always agree with your invoices and accounts

sections.title: Collect faster, owe nothing twice
sections.body: When a customer queries a balance, a clear ledger ends the conversation in seconds. When you need to chase, a statement of account is the single most persuasive document you can send. And because supplier ledgers track what you owe, you never pay a bill twice or miss a debit note. One reconciled record, for both sides of your business.

faqs.a: A running statement of every invoice, payment and credit note for a customer, ending in the balance they currently owe you.
Yes. Customer and supplier ledgers and statements of account are free forever on Paper & Pen.

faqs.q: Can I send a statement of account?
faqs.a: Yes. Generate a statement for any date range and send it by WhatsApp or email in one tap so the customer can settle.
Yes. Ledgers are built from the same entries as your invoices and payments, so the balances always agree with your accounts.

faqs.q: Can I see what I owe suppliers?
faqs.a: Yes. Supplier ledgers track bills, payments and debit notes so you always know exactly what is outstanding to each supplier.
